---
title: "Added Variable Plot"
description: "Isolate one predictor's contribution to a fitted model with an added-variable plot."
categories: [Regression, Diagnostics, Visualization]
---

::: {.callout-note title="Introduction"}
Use an added-variable (partial regression) plot to judge whether a predictor
still contributes once the other predictors are already in the model.
:::

```{r}
#| message: false

# required package
library(car)

# patient data carried over from the Multiple Linear Regression task
patient <- read.table("../../data/CH06PR15.txt", header=TRUE)

# fit linear model
gfit2 <- lm(Satisfaction ~ Age + Severity + Anxiety, data=patient)

# added variable plot
avPlots(gfit2)
```

Upon visual inspection of the added variable plots, it appears that $X_1$,
which represents age, may have a linear relationship with patient
satisfaction, while the relationship between $X_2$ and $X_3$ and patient
satisfaction remains unclear. It is possible these variables do not
significantly add value to the model.


## See also

- [Multiple Linear Regression](multiple-linear-regression.qmd)
- [Outliers & Influential Points](outliers-influential-points.qmd)

*Data: `CH06PR15.txt` — see [sources & provenance](../../data/README.md#regression).*
